article.title: Why Prospects Ghost After Sales Calls
article.subtitle: Evidence-backed patterns from real deal conversations

longTail: why prospects stop responding after discovery call

seo.metaTitle: Why Prospects Ghost After Sales Calls | Business Impact Canada
seo.metaDescription: Research-backed look at where momentum dies after sales calls—and how teams misread silence as interest instead of risk.

aeo.answerBlock: Prospects ghost when the next step is unclear, urgency is manufactured instead of mutual, or the buyer does not see a concrete reason to re-engage before your follow-up.

faqs.question: What is the most common early signal of ghosting?
faqs.answer: Slow or single-word replies after a strong live call, especially when scheduling slips without a replacement time.

article.intro: Ghosting is rarely random. It is usually the visible outcome of a breakdown that already happened in the conversation.

sections.heading: The credibility gap
sections.body: When claims outpace proof, buyers go quiet rather than argue. Silence becomes the safest move.

sections.heading: The next-step vacuum
sections.body: If the path forward is fuzzy, your follow-up feels like pressure instead of progress.

article.conclusion: Treat silence as a diagnostic signal. The goal is not more touches—it is a clearer reason to return.
